design_philosophy.core_loop: Practice → Qualifying → Race across full Formula One season with detailed car setup and damage simulation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing racing line precision, tire management, and mechanical reliability against AI competitors across iconic circuits
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic Formula One driver experience with granular control over vehicle setup and race strategy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Simulation-first design: no arcade mode; players must engage with full F1 weekend structure (practice, qualifying, race); extensive driving aids are optional restrictions rather than safety nets, positioning difficulty as player choice rather than game design gate-keeping

difficulty.checkpointing: Quickrace function allows jump into single race without qualifying; save/load system permits mid-season saves
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Five explicit difficulty levels with corresponding driving aid restrictions: higher difficulty tiers disable more driving aids (steering help, braking help, traction control, automatic shifting, racing line help). Players can also customize car setups with high granularity, directly affecting performance. Indestructibility toggle allows crash consequences to be disabled. This positions difficulty as player-controlled tuning rather than adaptive scaling.

progression.upgrade_system: Car setup customization system with extensive tuning options (suspension, aerodynamics, engine, gearbox, electronic parameters). Cars can accumulate damage during races affecting performance.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Championship mode progresses sequentially through 16 circuits of 1994 F1 season. Driver selection from 28 available drivers (replacing original pilot in their seat) provides role-playing progression.
replayability_hooks: Quickrace mode with customizable lap counts and grid positions
Replay save system for notable races
Car setup customization affecting race outcomes

world_building_techniques: Licensed 1994 F1 season authenticity (16 circuits, 28 drivers, 14 teams)
Historically-aware liveries reflecting non-tobacco regulation at 1994 German Grand Prix
Track design variations reflecting safety improvements made during 1994 season
TV camera angle option providing broadcast perspective

faction_indicators: Team selection (Williams-Renault, Ferrari, McLaren, Benetton, etc.)
Driver selection from competing teams creates rivalry identification
danger_signals: AI competitor aggression varies by difficulty level
environmental_storytelling: Weather absence (only dry conditions) reflects design era limitations
Absence of black flag system reflects arcade concession
Visual car failure simulation (smoke, flames) adds consequence visibility

sources_used: wikipedia: Grand Prix 2 gameplay section, teams/drivers table, circuits list, technical features
